ety: uṛ, rt. of uṛnā + S. नागिनी
def: s.f. A flying serpent; a dragon; (Met.) A she dragon, a woman in a violent passion.

ety: v.n. fr. عرس 'to keep or cleave to,' &c.
def: s.m. Marriage, nuptials; a marriage feast; a religious ceremony celebrating the union of the soul of a deceased pīr or saint with the Supreme Spirit (the saint's disciples gather round his grave, and read the fātiḥa, and offer food, incense, lights, &c., especially on the 12th of Rabīʻuʼl-awwal); offerings to a saint; oblations; obsequies
subs: (-kā), To celebrate the union (of), to give a marriage-feast; to celebrate the obsequies (of).

def: v.t. To cause emotion; to excite the passions.
part. adj. (f. -ī), Flying; on the wing
subs: lit. 'To know a bird on the wing'; (prov.) to know a rook when he is seen.

def: s.m. = اتو uttū, q.v.
adj. Large, great, wide, ample
s.m. Ascent; ascension; rising; exaltation; — zenith.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عُرُوج (urūj), from Arabic عُرُوج (ʕurūj). First attested in c. 1421 as Middle Hindi عروج ('rvj /⁠'urūj⁠/).

def: s.m. Raft; float; the moon (the half-moon being formed like a boat).
s.m. Fertile soil, yielding every kind of crop; land in general. (See ubarā).
= اربسي urbasī, q.v.
s.f. The earth.
= A عري ʻurī (v.n. fr. عري 'to be naked,' &c.), Naked, nude, bare, stripped, deprived (of), devoid (of).

ety: ʻuryān, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Nakedness, nudity, &c.

ety: S. तस्य, gen. sing. neut., base तद्
def: adv. That bank, the further or other bank (of a river); the other side
subs: adv. On that, thereupon; accordingly; on that account
adv. On that even; for all that; still; nevertheless
usī dam, adv. That very moment, instantly, forthwith
Under his name, to his address
For his name's sake, for his sake
adv. On that, meanwhile, in the meantime (for o her comp. v.s.v. is)
adv. Free, gratis, for nothing; without cause, reason or motive, gratuitously ( = yūṅ or yoṅ).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀏𑀲𑁄 (eso), from Sanskrit एषः (eṣaḥ).

def: v.n. = اکسنا ukasnā, q.v.
part. adj. Confused, disordered, tossed about; agitated
subs: v.n. To be tossed, to be disordered or confused: to be agitated.

def: v.t. To separate the husk or chaff from corn (usually done by driving or blowing it away with a fan), to winnow; to sift; to pour off or away ( = usevnā, q.v.).
v.t. To cause to boil; to boil. (Cf. ushannā — app. fr. rt. ush — and usannā, of which usānnā may be the caus.)
see اسننا usannā.
v.n. To boil; to simmer.
s.m. = اساس usās, q.v.
adj. Barren (land); — s.m. A barren spot, saline soil ( = ussar)
subs: usar-sāṅdā, s.m. A species of lizard found in sandy soil (see sāṅḍā).

def: s.m. Any passage for egress, porch, portico, peristyle, vestibule; verandah; shed.

ety: for A. عصارة, v.n. fr. عصر 'to press'
def: s.m. Expressed juice; extract; choice part; — dregs (after the juice is expressed), refuse

ety: S. अप, अव or उद्+सारणं, rt. स्ट
def: v.t. To remove, put out of place, displace; to finish, complete; to finish expeditiously.

ety: S. अप+सरणं, rt. सृः cf. the tr. form usārnā
def: v.n. To retreat, recede; to shrink from; to be removed or displaced; to be finished or completed with expedition.
s.m. Breath; breathing, respiration; sigh; groan.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اساس (asās), from Arabic أَسَاس (ʔasās).

def: v.n. To breathe, respire, to live, have existence.
s.m. A week.
s.f. Remembering with regret; grieving, pining or fretting (for), yearning (after); grief, vexation, anxiety, solicitude ( = auser)
subs: (-kī), To think (of) with regret; to grieve, pine or fret (for); to be anxious (about); to be grieved, vexed or displeased.

def: v.t. = اسانا usānā, q.v.: cf. also ulaćnā.
s.m. Safflower, bastard saffron, Carthamus tinctorius, or Cnicus.

ety: said to be fr. عصي وفرّ 'to disobey and fly'
def: s.m. The sparrow.
s.f. Morning light, dawn, daybreak, morning; the wife of Aniruddha, son of Kām-deva
wety: From Sanskrit उषस् (uṣas).

def: s. Gum ammoniac, galbanum.
s.m. Black pepper.
v.n. = اسننا usannā, q.v.
adj. Barren, &c. = ūsar, q.v.
s.f. = اشا ushā, q.v.

ety: for A. عشبة, n. of un. fr. ʻushb; rt. عشب 'to produce tender herbage'
def: s.m. Sarsaparilla.
s.m. Lit. 'That with which one is diverted'; tale, story, fable; fiction, fabrication, lie.
adj. Hot
subs: s.m. Lit. 'Heat-born'; any animal generated by heat (as gnats, flies, insects in a dunghill, &c.)

def: adj. Hot
subs: s.m. Lit. 'Heat-born'; any animal generated by heat (as gnats, flies, insects in a dunghill, &c.)

def: s.f. Heat, warmth ( = ushṇatā).
s.m. Heat, warmth; the hot season; — adj. Hot, warm; pungent, acrid.
s.f. Heat, warmth ( = ushṇa).
s.m. Turban; diadem.
s.m. A tenth part, a tithe
subs: The tithes prescribed by divine law
The tenth of a tenth, a hundredth part; (fig.) very little.

def: s.m. pl. (of ʻāshiq, q.v.), Lovers.
s.m. Camel ( = H. ūṅṭh; P. ushtur and shutur).
s.m. Fierceness, violence, taking anything by force, injustice; celerity; — adj. Firm.

ety: Pehl. ustar, Zend ustra, S. उष्ट्र
def: s.m. A camel ( = شتر shutur, the com. form).
v.t. To boil, to cook by boiling or simmering; — v.n. To be boiled, to be dressed or cooked over a slow fire ( = usijnā).

ety: S. उद्+सिचनं or perhaps from rt. श्री
def: v.n. To bubble up, boil, boil up; to simmer, to be boiled or cooked over a slow fire.
v.t. To boil, to cook by boiling or simmering; — v.n. To be boiled, to be dressed or cooked over a slow fire ( = usijnā).
s.f. A grass, the fragrant roots of which are used for making ṭaṭṭis, Andropogon muricatum (syn. ḵẖas-ḵẖas).
s.f. = اسير usīr, q.v.
s.m. Pillow, bolster; cushion; head of a bed ( = sirānā).
v.t. = اکسانا uksānā, q.v.
s.m. Order, arrangement; manner, mode, method; course, way, manner of acting or conduct; form, figure, shape (cf. ḵẖush-uslūb)
subs: adj. Methodical; well-arranged; well-proportioned, symmetrical; elegant.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic أُسْلُوب (ʔuslūb).

ety: perf. part. of اسننا usannā, q.v.
def: part. adj. Boiled
subs: s.m. Rice separated from the husk after being boiled.

def: = اسانا usānna, q.v.
s.m. A bull; a ray of light.

ety: for A. عسرة, inf. n. of عسر 'to be difficult'
def: s.f. Difficulty, hardness; intricacy; straitness; distress, poverty.
adj. = اوسر ūsar, q.v.
s.m. A barber (respectfully used for nāʼī).

ety: old P. vśtāt; Pehl. ǒśtāt; Zend aiwisti, rt. śtā = S. sthā+abhi
def: s.m. A master; craftsmaster, skilful man, adept, expert; preceptor, tutor, teacher; professor; clever rogue, sharper; tutor of a dancing girl, musician; — adj. Expert, proficient, skilful, clever
subs: To become or make onself master (of), to master, be or becom proficient (in).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اُسْتَاد (ustād). Doublet of اُسْتاذ (ustāz).

def: s.f. Teaching, instruction; mastery, preëminence, skill, excellence or skill in any craft or profession, expertness, dexterity, genius; finesse; strategy; master-stroke; subtlety, cunning, artfulness

ety: ustād+nī = S. aff. इनी or आनी
def: s.f. Schoolmistress, preceptress, teacher (of reading, writing and embroidery).
s.m. A barber (cf. ustā, and the next).
= استران ustarāṅ, q.v.
adv. Just that way, without cause, reason or motive; gratis, for nothing ( = يون yoṅ, q.v.).
= ustād, of which it is the Arabicised form.
Lit. 'Of the Stœchades' (islands); French lavender, sticados, Lavandula stœchas. (Also written اسطوقودس.)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اسطوخودوس (ustūxūdūs), from Arabic أُسْطُوخُودُوس (ʔusṭūḵūdūs), from Ancient Greek στοιχάς (stoikhás).

def: s.m. Bone; bones
subs: s.m. Splint or fillet for tying up a broken bone
s.f. Tying up a broken bone with a splint or fillet.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian استخوان (ustuxʷān).

def: s.m. A razor
= استرا usturā, q.v.
s.m. An astrolabe.
s.f. An astrolabe (see اسطرلاب ust̤urlāb).
s.m. A column
subs: s.m. A round column; a cylinder.

def: s.m. A column
subs: s.m. A round column; a cylinder.

ety: Pehl. ustuvār, Zend śtawrā, S. स्थावर
def: adj. Strong, powerful; stable, firm; even, level, equal; straight
subs: v.t. To strengthen; to make firm; to support, prop.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian استوار (ustuwār, “strong, firm”).

def: s.f. Strength, firmness, stability; confirmation, corroboration, support; resolution, constancy.
s.f. Roots and branches; causes and effects.
wety: From Arabic أُصُول (ʔuṣūl), plural of Arabic أَصْل (ʔaṣl).

def: = 1˚ اوسر ūsar; and 2˚ اوسارا usārā, q.q.v.
adv. There, thither.
s.m. A hut made of leaves; a hermitage.
Used in comp.: see next.

adj. & s.m. Given to random guessing; acting or speaking without consideration, thoughtless; rash, precipitate; conjectural, random, uncertain, wide of the mark; nonsensical, absurd; — one who is given to random guessing, a thoughtless or inconsiderate person, &c.
v.n. To jump, hop, skip, caper
subs: conj. part. Hopping and skipping, capering and dancing; — adj. Frolicsome; cutting extraordinary capers; extraordinary, strange.

r: utān, uttān, ā, ćit, aundhā, Lit.
def: = S اتّان उत्तान uttān, Adj. (f. -ā), Lying on the back; lying supinely on the broad of the back (syn. ćit); inverted, upside down (a vessel, &c.
subs: rugged; conceited, proud (Lit. "having the face upwards").

def: adj. = اتان uttān, q.v.
s.m. A species of nettle or stinging plant, the seed of which is used by the natives of India as an aphrodisiac, also the seed of this plant; — the name of a river near Gwāliyār.
adj. High up the leg, drawn up, too short (as trousers, &c.); shrivelled, shrunken; badly made or shaped.
adj. The lowest, least, most inferior (of, — se, e.g. Singāroṅ-meṅ jo sab-se hai wǒh utār)
subs: s.m. Descent and ascent, undulation (of land), ups and downs (of a road, or of fortune); decrease and increase, rise and fall, fluctuation (of prices, &c.); remission and exacerbation (of fever, anger, intoxication, &c.); ebb and flow (of a tide); higher and lower notes (of music); ascending and descending (scale); vicissitudes (of fortune); variation, alternation of hope and fear, or of loss and gain, or of good and evil, &c.; — shift, subterfuge, artifice, trick, fraud, deception
v.t. To make artful, wily, &c. suggestions; to cozen, deceive
v.t. To practise shifts, subterfuges, &c.; to plan, scheme; to cozen, cheat
s.m. Alleviation, relief, ease, comfort; remedy; discharge, liquidation (of a debt); fulfilment (of a vow, &c.).

def: s.m. Descent (to a river); crossing, passage (of a river or defile); ford, ferry, wharf; ferriage; dismounting (of cavalry to fight on foot); deduction, remission; — answer, reply ( = uttar); copy, transcript, duplicate; fac-simile, counterpart; halting-place, stage (of a journey), station (for travellers); — offering (to evil spirits, esp. masān, placed at the junction of cross-roads); delivering or freeing from demoniacal possession (by moving cooked rice, flour, &c. over the length of the body of the possessed); the articles used in the ceremony of dispossessing of an evil spirit; removing (a sickness) by drugs or charmed substances, (also) the substances employed for the purpose; ransom; self-devotion; land presented to a temple; grant of land (by Government) at a quit-rent in return for services rendered; house-rent; — cast-off clothes, second-hand or worn things
subs: v.t. To dispossess of an evil spirit by means of the utārā
(-par), To descend (to); to effect the passage (of a river, &c.); to dismount (as cavalry, to fight on foot)
s.m. A hut or shed for the accommodation of travellers; (Met.) the world; the body
s.m. Warehoused goods; second-hand goods, cheap things.

def: s.f. Descent; disgrace; fragment, &c. (see next, of which it is a verb. noun).
v.s.v. اتار utār.
s.m. The planet Mercury.

v.t. To cause to pass over, to convey over or across, transport over, ferry across, land; to cause to descend or alight; help to dismount; to bring down, take down, take off, remove, put away (from, -se); to tear off, strip off, cut or break off (a portion of anything); to lower, depress, abase, render vile, unclean or impure; to cast down, dethrone; to cast out, expel (an evil spirit); to draw down and confine (an evil spirit, in, -meṅ); to remove, or free from (fever, intoxication, &c.); to quit oneself of (a debt, obligation, oath, vow, &c.); to make a recompense or return; to unload, discharge (cargo, &c.); to disembark; to lodge, put up (a traveller); to throw aside, cast off (shame, &c.); to draw off (liquor); to make a channel (for water to flow down or off); to let down, lay (in a grave); to loosen the strings of, unstring (a guitar, &c.); to uncock (a gun); to vent (rage or spleen, on, -par); to extract (colour); to dispense, distribute, allot (sustenance); to send down (a prophet, or a gift, from heaven)
subs: v.t. To swallow or gulp down
v.t. To deprive of honour, disgrace, degrade, dethrone; to lower in the regard (of, -se); to put to shame, cause to blush; to put out (of caste); to put out, dislocate (a limb or bone
however, utār denā is identical with, or slightly intensifies, the verb utārnā)
v.t. To take off; take down, pull down; to take out, remove, rub out (colour, &c.; — cf. utārnā, of which utār ḍālnā is an intensive form)
v.t. (Intensive of utārnā, q.v. for other senses), to take down, enter, insert, record (in, -meṅ)

and utāre ho, part. adj. & adv. Dismounted; alighting, on foot; engaged with desperate resolution in any enterprise; determined, desperate (a metaphor taken from cavalry dismounting to fight on foot with a determination to conquer or die).
wety: Urdu verb set اُتَرْنا (utarnā) اُتارْنا (utārnā) اُتَرْوانا (utarvānā) Causative form of اُتَرْنا (utarnā) .

ety: utar˚ = Prk. उत्तर(इ) = S. उत्तर(ति), rt. उद्+तॄ
def: v.n. To pass over or across, go across; to come down, descend, alight, land, disembark, dismount; to make one's appearance (in, -meṅ, a place, or on a scene); to come into the world, have being, be born; to be incarnate; to be revealed; to be taken down, lowered, reduced, disgraced, degraded, deposed, dethroned; to decrease, abate, subside, fall off, wane, fade, decay, decline; to become old and worn, become wan or pale or thin; to fall (in price, value, estimation or dignity); to go down or off, sink, pass off (as fever, intoxication, &c.); to be delivered or freed (from debt, an obligation, an oath, a vow, possession by an evil spirit, &c.); to halt, encamp (in or on, -meṅ); to take (to, -par); to come out, turn out to be, to prove; to vary, change: (for other meanings see utar-jāna below). — utar-paṛnā (-meṅ, -par), v.n. To alight or settle (at or upon), to descend, come down; to fall (on or upon); to put up (in or at), to halt (at a place, on a journey). — utartā ćāṅd, s.m. The waning moon; the wane of the moon. — utar-jānā ( = utarnā in most of its meanings), v.n. To have got beyond (-se) middle age, to be growing old; to become faded, dim (as colour); to become pallid or emaciated or drawn (the face); to become insipid or flat; to be displaced or dislocated (a bone); to be taken down, pulled down, demolished; to pass away, die.
ex: گھوڑے سے اُتْرو اَور میرے ساتھ آؤ۔
ghoṛe se utro aur mere sāth āo.
Get off the horse and come with me.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀉𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀭𑀇 (uttaraï), from Sanskrit उत्त॑रति (úttarati), from उद्- (ud-) + तर॑ति (tárati).

def: adj. Disposed of, cleared, discharged, settled, paid off, balanced (as accounts).
adj. Descending, sloping, inclining, declivous; stooping, lowering (oneself); bent upon, determined; ready for
subs: (-par), To descend, incline, stoop (to); to be ready (for); to engage or embark (in).

def: v.t. To cause to be brought down, to be lowered, to be pulled down; to cause to be taken off, &c. (See utrānā.)
s.f. Haste, hurry, quickness, speed, despatch; precipitancy; eagerness, impatience; restlessness, fidgetiness
subs: v.n. To make haste, to hurry, &c.; to be hasty, rash, precipitate, &c.

def: adj. (f. -ī) & s.m. Swift, quick, speedy; precipitate, hasty, rash; uneasy, fidgety, restless, impatient
s.f. = اتاول utāwal, q.v.
(Braj) = (1) ات ut, and, (2), اتنے utne, qq.v.
(Prov.) = utnā, q.v.
s.f. = اٿهہ بيٿهہ uṭh-baiṭh. q.v.
v.s.v. اٿهانا uṭhānā.
s.m. Lit. 'Who picks up what is fallen or lying; pilferer, purloiner, petty thief.
part. adj. (from uthalnā), Upset, &c. The commoner form is uthal-puthal, or utthal-putthal, upset, capsized, overturned; topsy-turvy, in utter confusion, in complete disorder
subs: To be upset or overturned; to be topsy-turvy; to be disordered (the stomach).

def: s.m. One who is without any fixed abode; a person not to be depended upon.
v.t. = uthlānā, q.v.
subs: v.t. emphat. form of uthlānā, q.v.

def: v.s.v. اتهل uthal (2).

s.m. Area, court; — s.f. Act of appearing, rising, rise, ascension, ascent; height, elevation, eminence, altitude; top, summit; high range or flight; length, stature, tallness; make, build; springing up, growth; rising or swelling (of the breasts in girls); sexual desire, salacity (of the female); conceit or pride arising from sudden elevation (in rank, &c.); expense, outlay, expenditure
subs: adj. (f. -ī), Of high range or flight, soaring to a great height (as a bird).

def: v.t. To lift, take up, raise, raise up, elevate, hoist; to take up (anything held sacred) for the purpose of swearing by it, to swear by; to take up (one's effects, &c.) preparatory to moving or marching, (hence) to close work, break up; to put away, pack up; to start, set off, &c.; to rear, rear up, bring up, train, educate; to hatch, breed; to produce, invent, fabricate; to erect, build up, construct; to rouse (from sleep), to awaken; to support, bear, carry; to take upon oneself, bear the burden or responsibility of, undertake; to undergo, experience, suffer, endure; to incur (risk, expense, liability, &c.); to contract (debts); to obtain goods on credit (from, -se); to derive, acquire, get, gain, obtain (as the result of labour); to enjoy; to pick up; to gather in (as a harvest); to withdraw, remove (from, -se); to bear or carry forth or away, make off or away with, to steal; to cause to disappear or vanish, to consume, dispose of, dissipate, squander; to take away or remove (from the world), to translate; to clear or clean up; to finish, prepare; to efface, erase, expunge; to abolish, do away with; to eject, expel; to raise up (so as to be seen), to exhibit; to throw up, cause to rise or fly (as a paper-kite or a pigeon); to set apart, put aside (for any purpose)
subs: or baiṭhānā, v.t. To lead astray, cause to err; to excite, stir up, instigate
(intens. of uṭhānā), v.t. To lift up, raise up; to cause to vanish; to efface; to consume, dissipate, squander; to remove; to eject; to abolish; to make over, give, bestow, &c.
v.t. To take upon oneself, bear the burden or responsibility of; to put by
v.t. To take up, raise, lift up; to pick up; to take off (from, -se), to remove (from); to undertake the management or care of (see uṭhānā, of which uṭhā lenā is an intensive)
v.t. To run off or away with, to carry off; to abduct, kidnap.

ety: v.n. of उठंगना q.v.+ल = S. aff. आलू
def: adj. Dull, heavy, stupid, thick-skulled, deficient in sense or understanding, senseless, wrong-headed, silly, absurd
subs: s.m. Dull, stupid fellow, dolt, witling, dotard, a person devoid of all judgment, sense or discretion; a poor creature; a mere animal, a beast
uṭhangal-mulk. s.m. An ill-governed state or kingdom
a land of anarchy and misrule.

def: s.f. Dulness, heaviness, stupidity, &c. (see uṭhangal).
s.m. Prop, support; anything placed under a vessel to prevent its upsetting, or under a body to keep it from rolling; a scotch-stone, wedge, &c. (also written uṭhgan).
v.n. To rest (upon), lean (against), depend or rely (upon) for support; to be propped or supported; to be stopped or stayed.
s.m. Support; expenditure, expenses; running account (as with a baniyā); debit.
s.f. A certain ceremony performed among Hindūs in honour of a deceased person on the third day after death. (The corresponding ceremony among Mohammadans is termed پهول phūl, q.v.)
adj. & s.m. Extravagant, prodigal, lavish; — extravagant fellow, prodigal, spendthrift.
v.t. = اٿهانا uṭhānā
subs: (uṭhāʼonā), Something set apart to be offered to a god or goddess.

def: s.f. Rising up and sitting down, not sitting still; restlessness, fidgetiness, agitation; tossing and tumbling; perturbation, discomposure; a kind of exercise among athletes; standing and kneeling alternately at prayer; sitting and rising alternately holding both ears the while, — a mode of punishment in Indian schools
subs: v.n. To be constantly getting up and sitting down from uneasiness of mind; to suffer from restlessness; to have the fidgets.

def: s.m. = اٿهنگن uṭhangan, q.v.
part. adj. (from uthalnā), Upset, &c. The commoner form is uthal-puthal, or utthal-putthal, upset, capsized, overturned; topsy-turvy, in utter confusion, in complete disorder
subs: To be upset or overturned; to be topsy-turvy; to be disordered (the stomach).

def: adj. Shallow, low, shelving; superficial; unable to keep a secret.

ety: caus. of uthalnā, and i.q. uthālnā, q.v.
def: v.t. To invert, turn upside down, overturn, capsize, upset; to turn topsy-turvy, to throw into disorder or confusion; to toss, tumble, disarrange.

ety: S. उद्+स्था; pass. उत्थीय(ते), used actively; Prk. उट्ठे(इ)
def: v.n. To rise, rise up, stand up, get up (from bed, or from a sick-bed, — hence), to awake; to recover from illness; to ascend, mount, soar; to swell (as a river, &c.); to be inflamed (as the eyes); to be developed, to attain to puberty, to feel sexual desire (a female), to be in heat; to work, ferment (as leaven); to rise for the purpose of leaving (a place, — hence), to take leave, to leave, go away, set forth, come forth or be abroad (as a beast in quest of prey); to start; to move (from a house), quit, leave; to depart (from a place, or from this world); to be done away with, be abolished, be discontinued; to be removed, be carried away; to disappear; to be closed (as a shop or business); to be rubbed out, effaced, obliterated; to be laid out, expended, to cost; to be finished, be over; to be prepared, be ready; to be exhausted, spent; — to be raised, taken up, raised on high, (hence) to be exhibited; to come out distinctly, to appear (as type, &c.); to be erected, built, constructed, made, formed; to be reared, brought up, trained, educated; to be hatched; to spring up, sprout, shoot, grow; to proceed (from), originate, issue, emanate, result; to break out (as a sore, an epidemic, a mutiny, &c.), to arise (as a storm, a disturbance, &c.), to come on, begin (as pain, disease, &c.); to be started, be established (as any institution); to arise (out of), to accrue (as profit); to be derived, obtained, got, gained, realized; to be recovered; to be reclaimed or brought under cultivation; to be taken as a loan, or on credit, or on hire, or on lease; to be gathered in (as a harvest); to be drawn up, be absorbed (as ink by blotting-paper)
subs: v.n. To get up, sit up, rise up, start up, wake up; to be revived, to gain strength, to recover

v.n. (intens. of uṭhnā), To be removed, done away with, abolished; to come to an end, be expended, finished, terminated, settled, &c.; to cease; to remove, quit, go away (from, -se), depart (from this world); to vanish, disappear
To rise or stand up suddenly

v.n. To be constantly rising up and sitting down; to be restless, uneasy, fidgetty, &c.; to be constantly going out; to be in the habit of visiting; to associate (with); to do anything leisurely or at one's ease.

def: s.m. Deportment, manners, breeding; etiquette ( = P. nishast-barḵẖāst).
adj. (f. -ī), Rising, ascending; opening budding; developing; flourishing; — declining, waning fading, decaying
subs: adv. Lit. 'rising and sitting'; Easily, gradually, little by little; leisurely; slowly; — quickly, in a trice; incessantly, perpetually.

ety: from uṭhwā-nā; — iyā = S. aff. इक
def: s.m. One who rises, one who goes away; one who raises or helps to raise (a load, &c.); one who spends money lavishly, an extravagant person, a prodigal ( = uṭhāʼū).
v.t. To cause to be lifted or raised or removed, &c.
adj. Thrown off, cast off, worn (as clothes, &c.).
s.m. Thought, care; a porter, one who travels with a burden or load; a fowler; a name of the province of Orissa (Urīsā).
adj. (f. -ā), Having the neck uplifted; eagerly longing for; regretting, missing.
s.f. Longing for anything; regretting; uneasiness, care, sorrow.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Regretting; missing; wishing; sorrowing for; regretful, sorrowful, uneasy; — one who longs after an absent love.
s.m. = S اتکرشتا उत्कर्षता utkarshatā, s.f. Elevation, exaltation, eminence, excellence, superiority; distinction; excess.
s.f. = S اتکرش उत्कर्ष utkarsha, s.m. Elevation, exaltation, eminence, excellence, superiority; distinction; excess.
adj. (f. -ā) & s.m. Exceeding the usual measure, excessive; abounding in; drunk; mad; furious; proud; difficult; — intoxication; pride; an elephant in rut; Laurus cassia or its fragrant bark.
adj. (f. -ā), Superior; exalted; excellent, good.
= اتاولا utāwalā, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), As much as that, that much, so much, so many
subs: adv. (emphat. of utnā), Just as much as that, exactly so much or many
adv. As many as that, so many.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Causing, originating, producing; — originator, father; a certain fabulous animal with eight legs.
s.m. Producing, creating.
s.m. A species of blue lotus, Nymphæa cerulea; a certain plant (Costus).
part. Sprung, originated, produced, born, risen, resulted, gained; — s.m. Origin, creation, production, birth, rise, appearance; produce; proceeds; profit, gain
subs: To be produced, come into being, be born, &c.; to arise, result, occur.

def: s.m. Portent, phenomenon; distress; confusion; violence, injustice; injury, mischief. — utpāt karnā, To do wrong, violence, &c.; to contend; to be insolent; to answer, retort.
s.m. Plucking out, eradicating, &c. (see upāṛnā).
adj. & s.m. = ūṭ-paṭāng, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = ūṭ-paṭāng, q.v.

adj. Poor, helpless, miserable; useless, worthless; confused, in disorder; senseless, nonsensical, absurd, ridiculous; crabbed, hard to deal with, difficult to manage; — s.m. Anything without meaning or without foundation, nonsense, absurdity.
s.m. One who speaks inconsiderately or without preparation; a wretched creature; a senseless fellow.
s.m. = ūt-paṭāngī, q.v.
adj. (f. -inī), Violent, destructive, &c. (see utpāt).
s.f. Origin, production, birth, springing up; rise; title of the book of Genesis
subs: s.m. An epithet of the Supreme as the originator of creation

def: s.f. Indifference, carelessness; (in Rhet. & Poetry) comparison; illustration.

part. adj. Worn, aged, faded; dismissed from office; degraded, disgraced, &c. — utrā shahna mardak nām, prov. A dismissed superintendent of police is styled (or considered) a nobody, 'out of place out of grace.'
s.f. North wind (cf. uttarāhā).

s.f. Coming down, alighting; requital, return; price paid for carrying or conveying over (a river, bridge, &c.), ferriage, toll; decline of life; discharge (of a debt, &c.); draft, cheque, order (for payment of money)
s.f. A medicinal lactescent plant, Cynanchum extensum. (Also written utrang in Western and Southern India.)
s.f. A coming out (of), being freed (from); anything taken off from the body, worn-out or cast-off clothes; a fragment. — utran honā (-se), To be freed (from), quit (of), out (of debt). (See next.)

v.t. To cause to be taken off or down; to cause to be pulled down (in these senses اتروانا utarwānā is the common form); — to boil up or over; to froth, foam; to rest on the surface (of a liquid), to float: — s.m. A floating body; a float.
s.m. A wooden arch surmounting a door; the lintel of a door.
adj. (f. -ā), Freed from obligation, out of debt.
s.m. = اتسو utsav, q.v.
s.m. Joy, gladness, pleasure; labour, effort, perseverance, energy, fortitude.
adj. (f. -inī), Joyous, rejoicing; energetic, persevering.
s.m. The lap; the slope of a mountain; a roof.
s.m. Abandoning, resigning; giving; consecrating; a rule or precept.
s.m. Public demonstration of joy, rejoicing, festival; jubilee; — impatience, wrath.
adj. (f. -ī) and adv. That much or many, &c. (see اتنا utnā).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَتَا (atā), from a Turkic language.

def: adj. (f. -ā), Uppermost, highest, supreme, chief, principal, first, most excellent, best, greatest; last; great; high
subs: s.m. An excellent man, &c.; (in gram.) the first person.

def: s.f. Excellence; super-excellence; superiority; greatness; worth.

r: uttān, utān, ā, ćit, aundhā, Lit.
def: = H اتان उतान utān, Adj. (f. -ā), Lying on the back; lying supinely on the broad of the back (syn. ćit); inverted, upside down (a vessel, &c.
subs: rugged; conceited, proud (Lit. "having the face upwards").

def: adj. (f. -ā), Anxious, excited.
s.m. Placing one thing over another.
adj. (f. of uttar), Northern, northerly; to the north; — s.f. North wind, Boreas.
adj. (f. -ā), & s.m. Farther; posterior; last; lofty; upper; surpassing, superior; more powerful; excellent; best; left (not right); answering; succeeding, subsequent; future; northerly
subs: defence; superiority; power; arrival; the north; consequence; (in arith.) difference. — uttarādhikār (i.e. ˚ra+adhi˚), s.m. Inheritance, succession

adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Inheriting, succeeding; — heir, successor. — uttarārdh (˚ra+ardha), s.m. Latter half (of a book, &c.), last part; upper part (of the body); northern part; further end. — uttarāshāṛha, s.f. The twenty-first Nakshatra or lunar mansion, figured by an elephant's tooth or a bed, and containing two stars, one of which is β Sagittarius. — uttarāyana (˚ra+ay˚), s.m. The sun's progress to the north of the equator, northing; the summer-solstice. — uttara-bhādra-pad, s.m. The twenty-sixth lunar mansion, figured by a couch, and comprehending two stars, one of which is Andromedæ. — uttar-paśćim (or — paććhim, or paććham), adj. & s.m. north-west; the north-west

s.f. north-west wind; trade-wind. — uttara-phalgunī (or phālgunī), s.f. The twelfth lunar mansion, figured by a bed, and containing two stars. — uttar-dāyī (or-dāʼī), adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), answering; — one who gives a reply. — uttar-diśā, s.f. Northerly direction; the north. — uttar-kārī, adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), making answer; — one who replies. — uttar-kāṇḍ, s.m. The title of the seventh and last section of the Rāmāyan. — uttara-khaṇḍ, s.m. The concluding book of the Padma-Purāṇa, and of the Śiva-Purāṇa; — adj. Of Northern India. — uttara-lakshaṇa, s.m. The characteristics of a reply or defence (which are, 1˚ That it be applicable to the whole charge: 2˚ That it be maintainable by law: 3˚ That it be not equivocal or evasive: 4˚ That it be consistent with itself: 5˚ That it be perspicuously expressed). — uttarottar (˚ra+ut˚), adv. More and more; higher and higher; further and further; — s.m. Reply to an answer, rejoinder. — uttara-vartī, adj. (f. -inī), Existing in the north; northern.

def: For compounds of which uttara or uttar is the first member, v.s.v. اتر uttara.
adj. (f. -ī), Northern; appertaining to the North.
s.m. A place to which fish throng.
adj. (f. -ī), Northern; appertaining to the North.
s.m. An upper or outer garment.
s.f. = اٿهہ بيٿهہ uṭh-baiṭh, q.v.
adj. Shallow, low, shelving; superficial; unable to keep a secret.

s.m. A rising up, awaking from sleep; effort, manly exertion; battle; joy. — utthān ekādashī, or devotthān, The 11th of the śukl-paksh of Kārtik, when Vishnu awakes from sleep. During the months that he sleeps it is unlawful to marry.

ety: S. उद+स्थापन, caus. of rt. स्था
def: s.m. The act of raising a thing up, erecting, raising; tossing up; (in Arith.) producing an answer or result.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Landed, crossed, arrived; escaped, rescued, liberated; thrown off.
s.m. Raising, lifting.
s.m. Plaiting, puckering, crimping, gathering into ornamental plaits or folds; marking; — plaits or folds of cloth
subs: v.t. To streak, mark, or wale (the body by beating), to turn (one) into a mass of wales (by severe beating; — e.g. mārkar uttū banāya uske-taʼīṅ, 'he beat him to a mummy')

v.t. To plait, pucker, crimp, &c.; to streak or wale (the body by beating = uttū banānā); to make a fool of; to stupefy, besot; to overwhelm with raillery or abuse; to vilify, disgrace, dishonour, bring into disrepute
and uttū-gar, s.m. One who plaits, &c., one who makes ornamental folds in cloth
To become silly, or stupid, or stupefied, or besotted, &c.; to be disgraced, to lose repute or character, to fall into disrepute.

def: = P اتوکش uttū-kash, v.s.v. اتو uttū.
= P اتوگر uttū-gar, v.s.v. اتو uttū.
adj. (f. -ā), Tall, high, lofty.

ety: for A. عطوفة, fem. of ʻut̤ūf, v.n. fr. عطف; see ʻat̤f
def: s.f. Inclination; favour, kindness, affection ( = ʻat̤f).
s.m. Ploughing up a standing crop.
s.m. pl. (of ʻaib, q.v.), Faults, defects, vices, &c.
s.m. (f.?), Pomp, splendour; glitter; the habits or fashions of a king; the king's seal.

ety: intens. n. fr. عظم 'to be great'
def: adj. Very great, or great.
s.m. (f. -nī), s.m. A man of the Uzbek tribe of Tartars; a Tartar; an uncivilized man; clown, dolt, fool.

ety: for A. عزلة, v.n. fr. عزل; see ʻazl
def: s.f. Retiring; removal; retirement, withdrawal (of ooneself), secession; self-seclusion
subs: or ʻuzlat-guzīn, s.m. One who has made choice of retirement or seclusion, a recluse, hermit.

def: s.m. A hermit, recluse.
s.m. = ʻaz̤amat, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عظم ('azm), from Arabic عَظْم (ʕaẓm).

def: adj. f. (of aʻz̤am, q.v.), Greatest, supreme, the very greatest.
s.m. Excuse, apology, plea; pretext; objection; — the attainment or accomplishment of one's wants or wishes, success, fortunate issue
subs: v.n. To leave no ground for excuse or objection; to leave no objection unanswered
v.n. To bring forward, or to offer, an excuse or an apology; to plead; to raise an objection, to object (to)
adj. & s.m. Apologizing; apologetic; — apologist; one who begs pardon (of another)
s.f. An excuse, apology; excusing oneself for not attending a funeral; — condolence (syn. pursā); — ʻuẕr-ḵẖẉāhī karnā (-kī), To make excuse or apology; to excuse oneself (from); to apologize
adj. & s.m. Having a plea; producing an excuse; objecting; — an objector; a claimant
s.f. The having a plea, producing an excuse; a statement of objections; a cross-demand, a caveat
v.t. To make an excuse; to apologize; to take exception (to, -kī), to contest
(-kī), To bring forward an excuse, to plead excuses; to apologize; to raise objections (to)
or qābil-ě-ʻuẕr, adj. Excusable, pardonable; objectionable
s.f. Excuses, apologies; — ʻuẕr-maʻẕarat karnā, v.n. To make excuses; to make an apology; to pray (against, -kī), to deprecate

def: s.m. pl. (of ʻuẕr), Excuses, apologies.
prep. By (used in swearing, e.g. waʼl-lāhi, 'by God').
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian و (wa, u).

def: pron. (Braj) He, &c. (i.q. wah, q.v.); — him, &c. (i.q. us, obl. or format. sing. of wah)
subs: wā-teṅ, or wā-taiṅ, or wā-su, wā-soṅ, wā-sauṅ, or wā-se = us-se
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit वा (vā).

ety: prob. Zend apa; S. अप; cf. bāz
def: v.t. To open
subs: v.n. To be or become open; to open; to be freed or liberated; to be relieved of sorrow, to become cheerful.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit वा (vā).

def: prep. = با bā, q.v.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit वा (vā).

def: intj. (expressive of pain) Oh! ah! alas! alas for!
subs: intj. Ah! alas! (i.q. wā); — s.m. Weeping and wailing, lamentation, bewailing
or wā-wailā maćānā, v.n. To utter lamentation, to weep and wail, to lament, bewail; — to cry out, raise an outcry, to make a noise.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit वा (vā).

def: (cf. bā˚ in bāzār), s.m(?) Provisions; dressed food; a dish, mess (used in comp., e.g. gandum-wā, 'a dish made with wheat').
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit वा (vā).

ety: old P. utā; Zend uta; S. उत; — Ar. also وَ wa, 'and'
def: conj. And; too, also; with, in company with; but; now (when it merely connects two words it is sounded o, e.g. shab-ǒ-roz, 'night and day'; nishast-ǒ-bar-ḵẖāst, 'sitting down and rising up'; or 'he sat down and rose up').
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian و (wa, u).

def: conj. Or (i.q. athwā)
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit वा (vā).

ety: inf. n. of وبأ 'to be infected with the plague,' &c.
def: s.f. Plague, pestilence; epidemic disease; cholera morbus
subs: A plague, &c. to spread or prevail.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian وبا (wabā), from Arabic وَباء (wabāʔ).

ety: inf. n. of وبل 'to be heavy and unwholesome' (as air
def: adj. Unwholesome; burdensome; painful, vexatious; — s.m. An unhealthy climate or atmosphere; — anything painful or distressing; bane, pest, plague; — a crime, sin, fault; — punishment (for a crime); divine vengeance; curse; misfortune; ruin
subs: (-par), To be or become burdensome, or a plague (to one, — as life, &c.).

def: part. and s.m. contrac. of wā-basta, q.v.
part. Bound; restrained; — referred back (to); related, connected (with), depending (on); — s.m. A man-servant; a relative, or connexion; a dependant; an adherent.
s.m. pl. (of wā-basta), Domestics, dependants, connections, relations, adherents.
s.f. Connexion; dependence; adherence; obligation.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian وابستگی (wābastagī).

def: s.m.(?) Orris-root, Acorus calamus, or Zingiber zedoaria (cf. bać).
s.f. Speech, voice, talk, speaking, language, discourse; — a word, phrase, saying, adage, proverb; — assertion, asseveration, assurance, promise; — a name of Sarasvatī, the goddess of speech.
s.f. Orris-root (see wać).
s.f. Speech, &c. (i.q. vāć); — affirmation, assertion; agreement; promise, pledge; vow; oath.
s.m. Speaking, talking, saying, &c. (see vaćan); — a parrot.

adj. Speaking; — declaratory, explanatory, expressive (of), significant, implying, signifying; — verbal, expressed by words or sounds; — s.m. A significant sound, a word; (in Gram.) an explanatory particle; — a speaker; a reader; — a messenger.
adj. Talkative, garrulous, loquacious, talking much or idly, chattering, gabbling, prating; — boasting.
s.f. = S واچالتو वाचालत्व vāćālatva, s.m. Talkativeness, loquaciousness, garrulity.
s.m. = S واچالتا वाचालता vāćālatā, s.f. Talkativeness, loquaciousness, garrulity.
adj. = vāćāl, q.v.
s.m. The act of reciting or reading, recitation; — a preparatory rite in which the Brāhmaṇs invoke the blessing of the gods, &c.
s.m. Speaking; speech, word, &c. (i.q. baćan, q.v.); — (in Gram.) number
subs: (or baćan) pūrā karnā, To fulfil (one's) promise, to be faithful to (one's) word, — vaćan-kar, adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Acting according to orders, doing what one is told, obedient; — the author or enunciator of a precept
s.m. Order of words or speech, discourse
adj. & s.m. (f. -iṇī), Accepting the command (of), obeying orders, attentive to command, conformable, compliant, obedient, submissive, humble; — an obedient person, &c.
s.m. Mere words, empty talk, assertion unsupported by facts. (For other compounds see baćan.)
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit वचन (vacana).

def: adj. Expressed by words, verbal; textual; mentioned, expressed; — enjoined, prescribed.
s.m. (pl. nom. of S. vaćas), Words, &c. (the word occurs in the Rāmāyaṇ of Tulsī-dās).
adj. Talkative, garrulous, loquacious, talking much or idly, chattering, gabbling, prating; — boasting.
contrac. of next, q.v.

ety: pl. of wāćhiṛā, not in use; prob. Prk. वाच्छलिओ; S. वात्सल्य+कः
def: s.m. Praising, applauding; applause, commendations; — blandishments; affectionate and encouraging words; endearments; — cajoleries; — intj. Bravo! well done! aha! (syn. dhanya; wāh-jī).
adj. = vāćak, q.v.
adj. Communicated, or carried on, by speech or by word of mouth; verbal, oral; — s.m. News, tidings, intelligence
subs: s.m. News-letter, despatch, newspaper, intelligencer, gazette.

def: adj. (dialec.) = wāćrā, q.v.

ety: Prk. वाचडओ or वाचलओ; S. वाच+ल+कः
def: adj. (dialec.) Talkative, &c. (i.q. vaćāl); — absurd, nonsensical.

ety: prob. by redupl. of vać, q.v.
def: s.m. (dialec.) Twaddle, prating, babble, clack, gabble, nonsense (i.q. waṭ-waṭ).

ety: waćwaćā˚ = wać-wać, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि caus. aug.+nā
def: v.n. (dialec.) To babble, prate, twaddle, gabble, talk nonsense.
adj. & s.m. (dialec.) Babbling, talking nonsensically, twaddling, &c.; — babbler, prater, twaddler, &c.

adj. To be spoken or said, fit or proper to be said; — to be predicated (of anything), attributive, adjective; — expressible; — to be spoken against or gainsayed; blamable, censurable; contemptible, low, vile, bad; — s.m. That which may be said of anything; — blame, censure, reproach, abuse; — (in Gram.) a sentence; — a predicate; — voice (of a verb).
s.m. A son; — s.f. Wind (i.q. bād, q.v.).
s.m. Root, foundation, origin, source; matter (of anything).
s.f. = baṛā, q.v.

ety: for A. وعدة, n. of un. fr. waʻd, 'promising,' &c., inf. n. of وعد
def: s.m. A promise; vow; — an agreement, a bargain; an assignation, appointment; — a stated period (for the discharge of a debt, or the payment of a sum of money)
subs: To evade a promise or agreement
s.m. A false contract; — one who does not keep his promise (i.q. waʻda-shikan; syn. ʻahd-shikan)
s.f. Breach of promise or engagement; default
To make a promise; to promise, agree, engage; to make a bargain; — to make an appointment, to appoint
s.f. Place of assignation or appointment; — time of promise
s.m. Evading a promise; — promises, &c. (i.q. pl. of waʻda; e.g. us ne ham-se bahut waʻda-waʻīd kiye the lekin kućh z̤uhūr-meṅ na āyā)
adj. Keeping one's promise, true to one's word; punctual
(apnā), To fulfil a promise, to keep one's word or engagement
adj. = waʻda wafā, q.v.; — s.m. One who is true to his word

def: s.m. Speaking, talking; — sounding, sound; — speech, discourse; a word; a sentence; assertion; proposition; statement; — report, rumour; — explanation, exposition (of holy texts, &c.); — demonstrated conclusion; — an interlocution (for discovering truth); — a reply; — discussion, dispute, controversy; altercation, words; — an allegation, a plaint, an accusation
subs: (˚da+anu˚), s.m. Assertion and reply, attack and rejoinder, plea and counterplea, accusation and defence; a controversy, disputation, dispute, altercation
s.m. A formal disputation, assertion and counter-assertion, statement and contradiction; controversy
s.m. Proof of an assertion; the maintaining of an argument, controversy
s.m. Discussion (about any statement), argument and disputation, debate, dispute, argumentation, litigation
s.m. A war of words, logomachy, controversy, dispute.

def: s.m. = wadd, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Making a speech, speaking; saying, telling; — a speaker; — a musician.
s.m. A kind of silurus or sheat fish; — an eddy, a whirlpool.
(1st pers. sing. of the pres. Paras. of rt. vad), I speak, I say (the word occurs in the Rāmāyaṇ of Tulsī-dās).
s.m. The mouth; face, countenance; — (in Alg.) the first term (of a progression); — (in Geom.) the summit or apex (of a triangle, &c.
s.m. See s.v. vād.
adj. (f. -ā), Eloquent; speaking kindly, or agreeably, affable; — (for ava-dānya) liberal, generous, bountiful, munificent; — s.m. A liberal person, &c.
adj. (f. -ā), Munificent, liberal, generous.
s.m. (Dakh.) Babble, prate, jabber.

ety: wadar, q.v.+āṭ = āhaṭ = āwaṭ = Prk. अवट्टी = S. अ+वृत्तिः
def: s.f. Babble, &c. (i.q. wadar, q.v.).
v.n. (Dakh.) To babble, jabber, prate, talk nonsense.
s.m. Love, affection; friendship.
s.m. Killing, slaying; murder, slaughter, &c. (i.q. badh, q.v.)
subs: adj. Free from slaughter; uninjured by slaughter; preserved or rescued from murder.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Killing, destructive, murderous; pernicious, injurious
s.f. See بدهو badhū; and bahū
subs: (vadhu+aiś˚), s.m. The sway or supremacy of woman or women; petticoat-government.

def: s.f. = vadhūṭī, q.v.
s.f. = vadhūṭī, q.v.
s.f. A young woman; a young wife; — a son's wife, a daughter-in-law.
part. adj. See بدهيه badhya.
adj. Speaking, discoursing, declaring, saying, telling; — s.m. Speaker, &c. (i.q. bādī, q.v.)
subs: s.f., or vādi-prativāditva, s.m. The state or situation of plaintiff and defendant.

def: s.f. A valley, vale; low-lying ground; an oasis (in a desert); a desert; — channel (of a river); a river.

ety: for A. وديعة, v.n. fr. ودع 'to place, to deposit'
def: s.f. A deposit, trust, whatever is committed to another's charge
subs: s.f. The laying down of (one's) life, dying, death.

def: v.n. = bādnā, q.v.
adj. Loving, affectionate, friendly; — beloved; — an epithet of the Deity.
adj. (dialec. or rustic) = baṛwā = baṛā, qq.v.
s.m. A musical instrument (cf. bājā)
subs: s.m. A multitude of musical instruments; a band
s.m. A player on a musical instrument; a musician.

def: intj. Ah! alas! woe to; — fie!
subs: intj. Alas, alas! woe to!; — fie, fie!

ety: inf. n. of وفي 'to perform a promise,' &c.
def: s.f. Performing (or performance of) a promise, keeping an engagement; fulfilment; — observation of good faith, faithfulness, fidelity; sincerity; gratitude; — sufficing; a sufficiency; a completing or filling up; completion, conclusion, end
subs: s.f. The constant observance of good faith, fidelity
adj. Practising good faith, ever faithful
s.f. Faithfulness, fidelity, loyalty; sincerity, constancy

(-se), To keep faith (with), to be faithful, loyal, or true; to perform a promise, &c.; to fulfil; to satisfy; to come up to expectations; — to suffice (for); — to bear (with); to live in peace (with)
adj. 'Prone to fidelity,' faithful, &c. (i.q. wafā-pesha; wafā-dār)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian وَفَا (wafā), borrowed from Arabic وَفَاء (wafāʔ), from وَفَى (wafā). First attested in c. 1611 as Middle Hindi وفا (vfa /⁠vafā⁠/).

def: s.f. Fidelity, loyalty, constancy, sincerity (i.q. wafā)
subs: s.f. Breach of faith; want of fidelity, faithlessness, inconstancy.

ety: for A. وفاة, v.n. fr. wafā, q.v.
def: s.f. Death, decease, demise
subs: s.f. The death of Muḥammad; — an annual feast in commemoration of Muḥammad's death.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian وَفَات (wafāt), from Arabic وَفَاة (wafāh).

ety: for A. وافٍ wāfiṉ for wāfiyuṉ, act. part. of وَفَی 'to perform (a promise, &c.
def: adj. Faithful (to promises or engagements); sincere; — honourable; — complete, perfect, entire; full (weight, &c.), just (measure); sufficient; — plentiful, copious; numerous, many.

ety: act. part. of وفر 'to become full, or complete,' &c.
def: adj. Abundant, plentiful, exuberant, copious; — much, many; — rich, opulent, wealthy; — ample, large, vast; — s.m. A species of (Arabic) metre, 'the exuberant, the cæsura of which is mufāʻalatun (̆¯̆̆¯) six times repeated.

ety: for A. وافية wāfiyat, fem. of wāfī
def: adj. = wāfī, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of وَفِقَ 'to find (a thing
def: s.m. A sufficiency; a quantity just sufficient (for); — concord, accordance, agreement, congruity; — opportunity, convenient season (cf. muwāfiq)
adj. Full, complete, entire; numerous; copious, abundant (cf. wāfir).
(for A. وَغًي wag̠aṉ), s.m.(?) Tumult, clamour; war, battle.
adv. And the rest, et cætera, and so forth.
= H وگيري वगैरे wagaire, adv. corr. of وغيره wa-g̠aira, q.v.
= H وگيرا वगैरा wagairā, adv. corr. of وغيره wa-g̠aira, q.v.
conj. And if (cf. war-na).
s.m. = S واگجال वागजाल vāg-jāl, s.m. For this and other words beginning with vāg˚ that are not given below, see s.v. vāk, for which vāg is merely a euphonic substitute.
s.m. pl. of wāgā = bāgā, 'suit of clothes,' q.v.
s.m.(?) A species of metre.
s.m. Name of a caste who ensnare and catch wild animals; — a man of that caste; a hunter; a trapper.
s.m. See s.v. vāk, 'speech.'
s.m. = S واگدان वाग्दान vāg-dān, s.m. For this and other words beginning with vāg˚ that are not given below, see s.v. vāk, for which vāg is merely a euphonic substitute.
